untranslatable_reason: This signal requires multi-year baseline P&L tracking and period-over-period performance comparison (current P&L vs. historical average). The allowed indicators set contains only market price/technical data and does not include account performance metrics (equity curve, cumulative returns by period, or rolling P&L averages). The core logic—'exit when performance drops below 75% of 10-period historical average for 1 consecutive period'—requires portfolio/account-level data that cannot be constructed from price-based technical indicators alone.
translation_notes: Signal is a meta-rule about edge validity and regime change detection based on trading performance deterioration, not a price-action entry/exit rule. It requires access to trader equity curves and rolling performance statistics, which fall outside the scope of technical indicator-based backtesting. A proper implementation would need custom accounting functions in the backtest engine.
